approach: CR-A of a 3-way partition of issue #212, ratified live by the owner. Scope: the metaphor-free seat MECHANISM in packages/cyberlegion only. A standing owner record gains a PRESENCE pointer (`unit claim <handle>`) naming the live unit standing in for it; binding is gated on spawn capability by probing the multiplexer (never on an introspective 'is a subagent' carve-out); the presence resolves live-only so it can never name a corpse; mail/doorbell rings a bound live presence UNCONDITIONALLY and falls back to the focus-gated bound main pane when none is bound.

Additive-only on two frozen suites (unit/registry 10 added, mail/doorbell 5 added; gherkin-cli addOnly:true, 0 modified / 0 removed) -> both self-clear, stay @frozen, no re-open of any existing scenario.

Leash auto-spec not auto-all: this is the mail/identity machinery the whole fleet runs on, and the two load-bearing design decisions (mailbox handle `council`; presence SPLIT from the read-pane rather than reusing `attach`) were put to the owner live and ratified before drafting. The impl gate and the landing stay HITL — the owner ratifies at the PR.
floor: none — additive only; no frozen scenario narrowed, weakened, or deleted.

why.floor: none — 10 additive scenarios on unit/registry/registry.feature and 5 on mail/doorbell/doorbell.feature (gherkin-cli addOnly:true, 0 modified / 0 removed on both); both stay @frozen, no re-open. No narrowing, so the Clearance floor never fires.
why.blast: low — spec + suite only, no production code at this gate. Three nodes touched: unit/registry (presence pointer + spawn-capability gate + live-only resolution), mail/doorbell (presence-aware ring + focus-gated main-pane fallback), attach (README non-goal only, zero scenario change). The design composes existing neutral primitives (kind:'standing', probeMultiplexer, the ratified peer-vs-human ring split) rather than inventing machinery.
why.novelty: low — the split falls out of ALREADY-RATIFIED doctrine rather than contradicting it: mail/doorbell already rings a peer's pane regardless of focus because 'a peer is an agent expected to take the turn, not a human whose attention is the scarce resource'. A bound presence IS an agent, so it inherits the peer rule; the focus gate (#172) stays exactly where it was, on the human read-pane. Explore also corrected the issue's premise: the standing machinery already shipped — the real gap was that no presence pointer existed and the handle every Pod addressed had no standing record at all.
why.confidence: high — two cold sdd-spec-judge rounds. Round 1 ALIGNED false with 4 findings, ALL accepted and fixed (a literal 'seat' metaphor leak x2; an internal-call assertion; an unfalsifiable subagent scenario rewritten as a falsifiable 2x2 Scenario Outline; a pre-existing 'ship' leak on a line the diff had already turned into a +line). Round 2 fresh-context ALIGNED true, oracle/builder/architect all PASS, metaphor grep clean on every added line, addOnly independently re-verified by the judge's own tool run. Round 2's two content gaps: one closed (unit claim --clear on an unknown handle now specified — fail-loud wins over --clear's tolerance), one accepted as covered by the general statement.
why.judge: cold sdd-spec-judge round 2 — oracle/builder/architect all PASS; ALIGNED true; ship.
why.hitl: The two load-bearing design decisions were ratified LIVE by the owner before drafting (handle `council`; presence split from the read-pane). This gate is self-asserted within leash on that ratified shape; the landing stays the owner's at the PR.
